setting.note: A noir of the real altiplano capital: a provincial schoolteacher lands in the thin air of La Paz to chase a United States visa, gets stuck, and drifts down through the cheap hotels, pool halls, brothels, markets and cafes of the steep lower town. The map runs from the political heart at Plaza Murillo and the church of San Francisco down the Prado to the rough hostel quarter and out to the queue at the American embassy.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Juan de Recacoechea, first published 1994. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: American Visa, Juan de Recacoechea (1994). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The dried brick-red of the altiplano city, the colour of its adobe and its noir. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
